lenses.guidance: vv 2-15's repeated formula — 'if a man makes a vow' / 'if a woman makes a vow' — uses Hebrew neder (vow) and esar (binding pledge) as paired nouns. The literal force is sworn obligation; word becomes legal instrument by speech alone.

v 2's 'when a man makes a vow... he must not break his word' shapes the worshipper's posture toward speech. To pray today is to mean what is said; the heart that promises lightly cheapens its eventual obedience to the LORD.
